title: APIs and Async JavaScript
tagline: Fetch real data, master async/await, and build production-ready apps.

lessons.title: Fetch Data from APIs

accomplishments: Connect your code to real APIs on the internet
Fetch live weather data, user profiles, and more
Display dynamic content from external sources
Build apps that use real-time information

do.title: Build a Weather App with Real API Data
steps: Copy the code — a complete weather app
Enter a city name and click 'Get Weather'
See REAL weather data from OpenWeatherMap API!
Try different cities — it's pulling live data!
do.result: You fetched REAL weather data from an API!
do.proofRequired: showing weather data for at least 2 different cities

concepts.name: What is an API?
concepts.definition: Application Programming Interface — a way for your code to request data from another server. You send a request, the server sends back data (usually JSON).
concepts.analogy: A restaurant: you (code) are the customer, the menu is the API docs, your order is the request, the kitchen is the server, the food is the response.

concepts.name: How fetch() Works
concepts.definition: JavaScript's built-in function for HTTP requests. fetch(url) returns a Promise. .then(response => response.json()) parses the data. .catch() handles errors.
concepts.analogy: Ordering delivery: place order (fetch), get confirmation (Promise), wait (pending), food arrives (.then) or they can't deliver (.catch).

concepts.name: Promises
concepts.definition: Represent a value that will be available in the future. Three states: Pending (waiting), Fulfilled (got data, .then runs), Rejected (error, .catch runs).
concepts.analogy: A delivery tracking number — it represents food that hasn't arrived yet but will.

concepts.name: JSON
concepts.definition: JavaScript Object Notation — how APIs send data. Looks like JS objects but is text. response.json() parses it automatically.
concepts.analogy: A universal language all programming languages can read — like English in international business.

understand.keyTakeaway: APIs let you fetch real data from the internet. Use fetch() to request, .then() to handle response, .catch() for errors. Data comes as JSON.

build.hint: GitHub API doesn't need an API key for public data!

challenge.title: Build a Movie Search App
challenge.mission: Use the OMDB API to search movies and display poster, title, year, rating, plot, director, and actors.
requirements: Fetch from OMDB API with API key
Display movie poster, title, year, rating, plot
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add autocomplete suggestions as the user types.

capabilities: Understand APIs and how they work
Build API URLs with query parameters

bridge.preview: Great! You can fetch data. But there's a cleaner way to write async code — async/await makes it read like normal code.

lessons.devFession: I hardcoded API responses for a demo and forgot to switch to real fetch before the presentation. The CEO asked why the weather was always 72°F and sunny. In December. In Chicago.

lessons.title: Master Async/Await

accomplishments: Write asynchronous code that reads like normal code
Make async code cleaner and easier to understand

do.title: Convert Promise Code to Async/Await
steps: Notice they do the SAME thing, different syntax
do.result: Both do the SAME thing, but async/await is much cleaner!
do.proofRequired: showing movie data fetched using both methods

concepts.name: async keyword
concepts.definition: Makes a function always return a Promise. async function greet() { return 'Hello'; } — greet() returns Promise('Hello').
concepts.analogy: Putting a 'this is async' label on a function so JavaScript knows to handle it specially.

concepts.name: await keyword
concepts.definition: Pauses the async function until the Promise resolves, then returns the value. Only works inside async functions.
concepts.analogy: A pause button — 'wait here until the data arrives, then continue.'

concepts.name: try/catch for errors
concepts.definition: Replaces .catch(). Wrap await calls in try/catch for clean error handling. Familiar pattern from other languages.
concepts.analogy: A safety net — try to do something, catch it if it falls.

concepts.name: Promise.all() for parallel
concepts.definition: Run multiple async operations simultaneously. const [users, posts] = await Promise.all([fetchUsers(), fetchPosts()]). Much faster than sequential.
concepts.analogy: Ordering 3 dishes at once vs ordering one, waiting, then ordering the next.

understand.keyTakeaway: async/await makes asynchronous code look synchronous. It's cleaner, more readable, and easier to debug.

build.goal: Build a Multi-API Movie Search with async/await
build.hint: You can use async functions anywhere — even as onclick handlers!

challenge.title: Build a Recipe Finder with Multiple APIs
challenge.mission: Search recipes from TheMealDB API, display image, ingredients, and step-by-step instructions. Use async/await throughout.
requirements: Search recipes by name using async/await
Show multiple results in a grid
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add a 'Random Recipe' button using the random endpoint.

capabilities: Use await to pause for Promises

bridge.preview: Next: professional loading states and error handling — spinners, skeletons, retry logic.

lessons.devFession: I forgot 'await' before a fetch call and spent 45 minutes wondering why my data was always 'Promise { <pending> }'. One missing word. 45 minutes.

lessons.title: Handle Loading States and Errors

accomplishments: Show users when data is loading (no more blank screens!)
Handle errors gracefully with helpful messages
Add retry functionality when requests fail
Build professional UIs that feel polished

do.title: Build a Professional Loading System
steps: Copy the code — 6 different loading patterns
Click each button to see: spinner, skeleton, dots, error with retry, progress bar, network error
Each gives the user clear feedback
do.result: You saw 6 professional loading patterns: spinner, skeleton, dots, error+retry, progress, network error!
do.proofRequired: showing at least 3 different loading states working

concepts.name: Why Loading States Matter
concepts.definition: Users need feedback. Without indicators, they think your app is broken. 0-100ms = instant. 100ms-1s = brief indicator. 1s-10s = progress. 10s+ = progress + time estimate.
concepts.analogy: An elevator without a floor indicator — you'd think it's stuck.

concepts.name: Skeleton Screens
concepts.definition: Gray placeholder shapes that mimic the layout while loading. Better than spinners because they show what's coming.
concepts.analogy: A blueprint of a house while it's being built — you can see the structure before it's finished.

concepts.name: Retry Logic
concepts.definition: Automatically retry failed requests with exponential backoff: 1st try immediate, 2nd wait 1s, 3rd wait 2s, 4th wait 4s.
concepts.analogy: If someone doesn't answer the phone, you wait a bit longer each time before calling again.

concepts.name: Timeout Handling
concepts.definition: AbortController cancels requests that take too long. Prevents infinite waits. Set 5-10s for API calls, 30s for uploads.
concepts.analogy: Setting a timer when waiting for food delivery — if it takes too long, you cancel and order elsewhere.

understand.keyTakeaway: Professional apps show loading states, handle errors with helpful messages, and retry automatically. Never leave users confused.

build.goal: Build a Professional Data Dashboard with independent loading states per section

codeComparison.old:
// No loading state
const data = await fetch(url);

build.hint: Each section has its own loading state. They don't block each other!

challenge.title: Build a Robust Image Gallery
challenge.mission: Gallery with skeleton loading, individual image error handling, retry with backoff, rate limit handling, progress indicator, and timeout.
requirements: Specific error messages per error type
Load More button with loading state
challenge.bonusChallenge: Show banner when 3+ images fail: 'Having trouble loading. Check your connection.'

capabilities: Set timeouts to prevent infinite waits

bridge.preview: Final lesson: combine everything into a complete, production-ready application!

lessons.devFession: I deployed an app with no loading states. Users clicked the button, saw nothing for 3 seconds, clicked again, and again. Each click fired a new API call. The server got 47 requests for the same data.

lessons.title: Build a Complete API-Powered App

accomplishments: Build a complete cryptocurrency tracker with live data
Combine multiple APIs into one cohesive application
Create a real-world app you can actually use

do.title: Build a Live Cryptocurrency Tracker
steps: Notice: caching, auto-refresh, error handling — all production patterns
do.result: A COMPLETE, production-ready crypto tracker with caching, auto-refresh, search, and error handling!
do.proofRequired: showing the crypto tracker with live prices and market stats

concepts.name: API Caching
concepts.definition: Store responses temporarily to avoid redundant requests. Check timestamp — if fresh enough, return cached data instead of fetching.
concepts.analogy: Checking your fridge before going to the grocery store — no need to buy milk if you already have some.

concepts.name: Auto-Refresh
concepts.definition: Update data at regular intervals. setInterval for polling. Pause when tab is hidden (saves battery/bandwidth). Let users toggle it.
concepts.analogy: A news ticker that updates every 30 seconds — but pauses when you walk away from the TV.

concepts.name: Data Transformation
concepts.definition: Convert raw API data into display-friendly formats: 1234567890 → '1.23B', timestamps → 'Just now', percentages → colored badges.
concepts.analogy: Raw ingredients → a plated dish. Same data, different presentation.

concepts.name: Search/Filter on Cached Data
concepts.definition: Filter client-side for instant results. allData.filter(item => item.name.includes(searchTerm)). No API call needed.
concepts.analogy: Searching your bookshelf vs going to the library — local search is instant.

understand.keyTakeaway: Production apps cache intelligently, auto-refresh responsibly, transform data for display, and degrade gracefully when things fail.

build.goal: Extend the crypto tracker with favorites and charts
build.hint: Use localStorage to persist favorites across page refreshes.

challenge.title: Build a News Aggregator
challenge.mission: Aggregate from multiple news APIs, deduplicate, sort, filter, cache, and auto-refresh.
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add 'Save for Later' with localStorage bookmarks.

capabilities: Build search and filter on cached data

bridge.preview: Module 5 complete! You can now build real applications that fetch live data. Next: DOM Manipulation Mastery — create elements, animate, and build interactive components!

lessons.devFession: I built a crypto tracker that refreshed every second. The API rate-limited me after 2 minutes. 30-second intervals with caching is the sweet spot.
